file_insights.purpose: Initialize Django management package for menu app
Django management command for seeding EU 14 standard allergens
key_features: Comprehensive docstring with allergen reference table
Duplicate detection by both code and slug
Structured allergen data following EU FIC 1169/2011
Detailed console output with visual indicators (+, -, ~)

patterns_discovered.description: Standard Django pattern using BaseCommand with add_arguments, handle, and private helper methods
patterns_discovered.evidence: Command class with add_arguments() and handle() methods, _create_allergens() helper

patterns_discovered.description: Comprehensive documentation of EU FIC 1169/2011 allergen regulations with reference table and usage examples
patterns_discovered.evidence: Detailed docstring with allergen code table, 14 standard allergens, legal framework reference

patterns_discovered.description: Seed command designed to be run multiple times safely with duplicate detection and optional force update
patterns_discovered.evidence: Check existing by code and slug, conditional create vs update logic, --force flag

patterns_discovered.description: Implementation of dry-run mode using custom exception to rollback atomic transaction
patterns_discovered.evidence: DryRunException class raised within atomic block to trigger rollback without database changes

patterns_discovered.description: Seed data defined as list of dictionaries with consistent schema including code, name, slug, description, sort_order, is_active
patterns_discovered.evidence: allergens_data list with 14 consistently-structured allergen definitions

patterns_discovered.description: Console output uses symbols (+, -, ~) combined with styling to provide clear operation feedback
patterns_discovered.evidence: Created (+ symbol), Skipped (- symbol), Updated (~ symbol) with style.write() calls

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Duplicate detection requires checking both code and slug fields
gotchas_discovered.description: The command checks for existing allergens by both code AND slug to ensure uniqueness across different field combinations
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Query both fields and use OR logic to find any existing record before deciding to create or update

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Dry-run mode uses exception-based control flow within atomic transaction
gotchas_discovered.description: DryRunException is raised and caught to trigger transaction rollback, which is unconventional control flow
gotchas_discovered.consideration: This approach works but could be confusing; alternative would be to avoid entering atomic block in dry-run mode

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Force update overwrites all provided fields indiscriminately
gotchas_discovered.description: When --force flag is used, all fields in the data dictionary are updated without selective field handling
gotchas_discovered.consideration: This could overwrite customized values if allergen records were manually edited post-seeding

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: EU 14 allergens are hard-coded as platform-level entities
gotchas_discovered.description: Allergens are noted as not tenant-specific but the command doesn't explicitly handle multi-tenancy if present
gotchas_discovered.consideration: May need adjustment if multi-tenancy is implemented in the future

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created a well-structured Django management command for seeding EU 14 standard allergens
key_accomplishments: Implemented complete Django management command with proper structure and inheritance
Comprehensive documentation including regulatory reference and usage examples
Flexible seeding with create, update, and dry-run capabilities
Robust duplicate detection by multiple fields
User-friendly console output with visual feedback
All 14 EU allergens properly defined with codes, descriptions, and sort ordering
approach_outcome.session_efficiency: Completed in single attempt with comprehensive implementation

recommendations.recommendation: Consider alternative to exception-based dry-run rollback
recommendations.rationale: Using exceptions for control flow is unconventional; could use conditional logic to avoid atomic block in dry-run mode for clarity

recommendations.recommendation: Add confirmation prompt before executing with --force flag
recommendations.rationale: Force update affects existing records; a confirmation prompt would prevent accidental overwrites

recommendations.recommendation: Add examples of incorrect allergen configurations to watch out for
recommendations.rationale: Could help future maintainers understand field requirements (e.g., code format, slug format)

recommendations.recommendation: Create unit tests covering all three paths: create, update, skip
recommendations.rationale: Command logic has multiple branches that should be tested independently (create new, update existing, skip existing)

recommendations.recommendation: Consider parameterizing allergen definitions from external source (JSON/YAML file)
recommendations.rationale: Current hard-coded approach works well, but external source would make updates easier without code changes

recommendations.recommendation: Add summary statistics at the end (created: X, updated: Y, skipped: Z)
recommendations.rationale: Would provide better visibility into operation results at a glance
